lobbying_activities: 20th May 2025.  CEO invited Minister James Brown to visit St Michael's House - Letter
30 June 2025.  Letter from CEO to list of TD's Ministers advocating for Disability Services in Budget 2026. - Letter

31st July 2025.  CEO and Director of Access, Integration & Adult Services met with Minister Chambers to discuss funding and budgetary concerns for St Michael's House and disability services. - Meeting
June 2025. Official opening of Caislean Day Services.  A day services for adults with disabilities. - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Invitation to TD's to visit St Michael's House (December 2024)
records.intended_results: Welcoming TD's to visit St Michael's House or have Teams meeting to discuss the challenges within the disability sector and potential solutions.  It is a fantastic opportunity to work together to create positive change for individuals with disabilities and their families and carers.
lobbying_activities: Letters sent to all current TD's in December 2024.  Letter included manifesto produced by National Federation of Voluntary Service Providers. - Letter

records.specific_details: Pre-Budget Communication to the Seanad, Ministers and TD's highlighting the issues facing people with intellectual disabilities and their families, and the critical sustainability issues that are facing the organisations providing supports for these individuals - this includes St Michael's House.
records.intended_results: In Budget 2025, it is essential that the Government commits to fully resourcing and implementing the Action Plan for Disability Services 2024-2026.
lobbying_activities: Letters were sent to all Senators, TD's and Ministers. - Letter

records.specific_details: Annual National Household Travel Survey
records.intended_results: To reconsider proceeding with this outdated method, https://www.etenders.gov.ie/epps/cft/prepareViewCfTWS.do?resourceId=5530425 I propose leveraging anonymized mobile phone GPS tracking data combined with AI-driven analysis to create a more robust, scalable, and real-time solution for transport planning and management. ::Educate TDs on the near-future possibilities with AI traffic management, as mentioned during the media debate about Metrolink, highlighted by Dermot Desmond at an AI conference in Belfast https://www.irishtimes.com/ireland/dublin/2025/08/05/dublins-planned-metrolink-will-be-obsolete-because-of-artificial-intelligence-says-dermot-desmond/

records.specific_details: Bereaved Partner’s Bill 2025
records.intended_results: To seek support for FLAC's proposed amendments to the 2025 Bill
lobbying_activities: Additional info: Follow up email to Senator Eileen Flynn on 8 July 2025 signaling our availability to discuss the legislation with her. - Email
Direct emails sent to members of the Oireachtas on 26 June 2025 to invite members of the Oireachtas to a FLAC briefing concerning the 2025 Bill. - Email

Email of 14 May 2025 to Dara Calleary TD, Minister for Social Protection, following up on earlier letter sent by FLAC seeking a meeting with him to seek the Minister's support for FLAC's proposed amendments to the 2025 Bill. - Email
Email sent to all members of Seanad Eireann on 8 July 2025 to seek Senators' support for FLAC's proposed amendments to the 2025 Bill. - Email

Email to the Chair of the Oireachtas Social Protection Committee, John Paul O’Shea TD, dated 13 May 2025 seeking an opportunity to engage with the Committee to seek support for FLAC's proposed amendments to the 2025 Bill. - Email
Follow-up email to Louise O’Reilly TD on 13 May 2025 with proposed parliamentary question concerning the 2025 Bill. - Email
Follow-up email to the Chair and members of the Oireachtas Social Protection Committee on 24 June 2025 (after FLAC appeared before the Committee the week before) - Email

Follow-up emails to Louise O’Reilly TD seeking support and assistance with an AV Room Briefing concerning the 2025 bill on 22 May 2025, 12 June 2025, 13 June 2025, 25 June 2025 and 26 June 2025. - Email
Meeting with Dara Calleary TD, Minister for Social Protection, in Leinster House on 19 June 2025 to discuss FLAC's proposed amendments to the 2025 Bill. - Meeting
Meeting with Louise O'Reilly TD in Leinster House on 8 May 2025 to seek the deputy's support for FLAC's proposed amendments to the 2025 Bill - Meeting

records.specific_details: Liaison on the upcoming launch of the Real Face of Men's Health report which makes clear the need for the government to progress the National Men's Health Action plan, with a range of recommendations on priority areas including the need for cross government support statement and clarity on initial funding for the action plan.
records.intended_results: TDs and Ministers show their public support for action on men's health with statements in the Dáil, attendance at events and meetings with Movember and progression of the national men's health action plan as per the recommendation in the Real Face of Men's Health report available here https://ie.movember.com/movember-institute/the-real-face-of-mens-health-report
lobbying_activities: Danny has been contacting TDs and the team for the Minister of Health to arrange meetings ahead of launch and share information on the report - Email

records.specific_details: The Wheel hosted launch event for our Pre-Budget Submission 2026. It comprised a panel discussion with speakers from community and voluntary organisations and an overview of The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026. We invited Ministers, TDs and Senators from the most relevant departments and joint Oireachtas committees and several attended in person.
records.intended_results: To inform TDs and Senators about The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026, which includes issues such as sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.

records.specific_details: Emails to Members of the Oireachtas Committee on Social Protection, Community & Local Development requesting to put combatting poverty at the top of the committees agenda, with particular reference to income adequacy, accessible public services & measures targeted at the most disadvantaged groups
records.intended_results: To influence decision makers to be more conscious of the impact of poverty on the individuals affected and society as a whole by providing information and research in relation to legislation and policy in this area with particular regard to the area of income adequacy, availability of public services, and targeted measures for the most disadvantaged gorups
lobbying_activities: Email contact with Chair & Members of the Committee for Social Protection, Community & Rural Development requesting contact in relation to the work of the committee in pre-budget preparation, the need for income adequacy, the new Anti-Poverty strategy and the need to add socio-economic disadvantage as a ground for discrimination in Equality legislation - Email

Email request to Liam Quaide TD & Mark Wall TD to submit Parliamentary Questions in relation to income adequacy, the need for socio-economic status to be included as a ground for discrimination under the equality act. - Email
Email to Luke Ming Flanagan MEP & Michael McNamara requesting a visit to the European Parliament - Email

Email to the chair and individual members of the Committee on Social Protection who had responded to our letter of introduction, regarding placing the Anti-poverty Strategy on the work programme of the committee for the autumn Liam Quaide, Mark Wall, Pat the Cope Gallagher & Catherine Ardagh - Email

Follow on meeting with Liam Quaide TD and the assistant of Mark Wall TD in Leinster House 17/6 in relation to their work as Social Protection Spokespersons for their parties and the work of the committee - Meeting
Submission of Amendments to Aodhan O'Riordan MEP as part of the Eruopean Parliament's Proposals for the EU Anti-poverty Strategy - Email

Submission to the committee on social protection pre-budget scrutiny. Submission to the joint committee on COM(2025) 123 Proposal for a regulation of the European Parliament and of the Council amending EU Regulations (EU) 2021/1058 and EU 2021/1056 as regards specific measures to address strategic measures as part of mid term review - Submission

records.specific_details: Sent Pre-Budget Submission to the Joint Oireachtas Committee on Social Protection. This Submission was invited by the Committee as part of the annual Pre-Budget process. We have registered this lobbying with each member of the Committee out of an abundance of caution.
records.intended_results: i) Publish a timeline for implementation of the Programme for Government Actionto abolish the means test for Carers Allowance within the lifetime of thisGovernment.  ::i) Increase the weekly limit for education, work and voluntary hours for those inreceipt of Carers Allowance, Carers Benefit, and Carer Support Grant. Werecommend a minimum of 20 hours per week.ii) Examine the current methods for calculation of the permissible working hoursto take account of once-off, seasonal work available to family carers. ::i) Raise the levels of payments for Carers Allowanceii) Support the recent ask from the Oireachtas Disability Group, calling for a Costof Disability payment of €55 per week (€2, 860 per annum)iii) Raise the level of the Carer Support Grant to €2,060, in line with the projectedaverage wage increase in 2026.
lobbying_activities: Email

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: Relaunch and Coordination of the All-Party Oireachtas Group on Dementia
records.intended_results: Relaunch the All-Party Oireachtas Group on Dementia for the new Oireachtas term - which The Alzheimer Society of Ireland provides the secretariat to - in order to enhance collaboration on dementia policy and improve the lives of people with dementia and their carers

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Agriculture, Food and the Marine

lobbying_activities: Coordination meeting for the All-Party Oireachtas Group on Dementia. Zoom call on the 18th August with Co-Chairs of the Group, Senator Fiona O'Loughlin and Deputy George Lawlor. Discussion on workplan for the Group and welcoming new members to the Group in the future. - Virtual Meeting

Initial kick-off and coordination meeting for the relaunched All-Party Oireachtas Group on Dementia for the new Oireachtas term. This was a Zoom call on the 15th May with Chair of the Group, Senator Fiona O'Loughlin. - Virtual Meeting

The All-Party Oireachtas Group on Dementia, inaugural meeting, for the new Oireachtas term which took place in Leinster House on 18th June. This meeting was attended by a number of TDs and Senators, which are listed below. Wide ranging discussion on developing a workplan for the group, possible AV Room events in Leinster House, dementia community supports and services and agreeing new Co-Chairs of Senator Fiona O'Loughlin and Deputy George Lawlor. - Meeting

records.specific_details: Pre Budget Submission 2026
records.intended_results: Support for LDCN's Budgetary asks in Budget 2026.
lobbying_activities: LDCN presented their Pre-Budget Submission to the Joint Oireachtas Committee on Social Protection, Community and Rural Development on the 16th July. - Meeting

records.specific_details: Wrote to the Joint Committee on Social Protection
records.intended_results: To ask if a review of the Cost of Disability for those on the State Pension will be considered in the workplan.
lobbying_activities: The Joint Committee will write later in the year to invite Polio Survivors Ireland to submit evidence. - Email

records.specific_details: Stakeholder meetings and emails in relation to the Heat Bill and SSRH and other grant funding.
records.intended_results: Policy and grant supports for District Heating in Ireland, in order to support hitting the government emissions reduction targets for heat

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Environment, Climate and Communications

lobbying_activities: 11.04.2025Sent a stakeholder document with reflections on the Heat Bill to Brian Carroll - Email
Email to Directly Elected Mayor of Limerick, John Moran 2nd April 2025Sent an email introduction regarding the potential for district heating in Limerick and seeking a meeting - Email

Had a meeting with Deputy Ciaran Ahern 10th March 2025Discussed the current status of the Heat (Networks and Miscellaneous Provisions) Bill 2024 (Heat Bill) and SSRH and INCF funding for District Energy. - Meeting

Had a phone call with Minister Hildegarde Naughton 8th May 2025Discussed the potential for district heating for Galway, the current status of the Heat (Networks and Miscellaneous Provisions) Bill 2024 (Heat Bill) and SSRH and INCF funding for District Energy. - Phone call

Had a series of meetings in Leinster House 9th April 2025 - Sean Kyne, Catherine Ardagh, Erin McGreehan, Ollie Crowe, Roderic O'Gorman, Barry Ward, Albert DolanDiscussed the current status of the Heat (Networks and Miscellaneous Provisions) Bill 2024 (Heat Bill) and SSRH and INCF funding for District Energy. - Meeting

Leinster House, 9th April 2025Meeting with Assistant Secretary Brian Carroll to introduce new CEO and discuss the Heat Bill, stakeholder reflections on the bill and upcoming funding under the ICNF and NDPDiscussed the current status of the Heat (Networks and Miscellaneous Provisions) Bill 2024 (Heat Bill) and SSRH and INCF funding for District Energy. - Meeting

Meeting in Galway with Deputy John Connolly 11th April 2025Discussed the potential for district heating for Galway, the current status of the Heat (Networks and Miscellaneous Provisions) Bill 2024 (Heat Bill) and SSRH and INCF funding for District Energy - Meeting

Meeting with Aine Doyle April 23rd 2025Meeting with Special Advisor to Minister Darragh O'Brien to introduce new CEO and discuss the Heat Bill, stakeholder reflections on the bill and upcoming funding under the ICNF and NDPDiscussed the current status of the Heat (Networks and Miscellaneous Provisions) Bill 2024 (Heat Bill) and SSRH and INCF funding for District Energy. - Meeting
Meeting with Senator Mark Duffy 15th April 2025Discussed the Heat Bill and funding for district heating and the opportunities for district heating in Mayo. - Meeting

Met with Deputy Pa Daly 11th February 2025Discussed the current status of the Heat (Networks and Miscellaneous Provisions) Bill 2024 (Heat Bill) and SSRH and INCF funding for District Energy. - Meeting
